references.name: NHS App web integration overview
references.borrowed: Treat the NHS App as a tailored webview with native header and app-specific functionality.
references.rejected: Did not duplicate native app chrome inside the route.

references.name: NHS App web integration guidance
references.borrowed: Site links, NHS App traffic hints, invalid session pages, and webview file download limitations informed recovery and fallback states.
references.rejected: Query hints are not treated as trust or eligibility signals.

references.name: NHS App JavaScript API v2
references.borrowed: Bridge navigation and download-from-bytes behavior informed deliberate progress and transfer states.
references.rejected: No byte issuance or bridge invocation was implemented in this frontend task.

references.name: NHS App accessibility statement
references.borrowed: Known app web-content keyboard and document accessibility constraints informed summary-first fallback.
references.rejected: No inaccessible document preview is treated as the only route to information.

references.name: NHS service manual error message guidance
references.borrowed: Error copy focuses on recovery and next action instead of technical failure.
references.rejected: Recovery and permission states are not styled as form validation errors.

references.name: NHS App page not found pattern
references.borrowed: Plain language, short error content, and urgent fallback awareness.
references.rejected: The task does not use a generic 404 pattern when route context can be preserved.

references.name: WAI-ARIA Authoring Practices Guide
references.borrowed: Use HTML semantics, landmarks, accessible names, and widget patterns deliberately.
references.rejected: No custom ARIA widget was introduced where native sections, buttons, and lists are sufficient.

references.name: WAI-ARIA main landmark example
references.borrowed: One main landmark and labelled regions for primary content.
references.rejected: Did not add multiple unlabeled main landmarks.

references.name: Playwright ARIA snapshots
references.borrowed: ARIA snapshot assertions are used for recovery and artifact cards.
references.rejected: Large full-page ARIA snapshots are avoided where targeted assertions are clearer.

references.name: Playwright emulation
references.borrowed: Narrow embedded-width testing uses device-like viewport, touch, and user agent settings.
references.rejected: Desktop-only verification is not accepted for this route family.

references.name: Playwright trace viewer
references.borrowed: Trace capture is used for return-safe and artifact fallback transitions.
references.rejected: Trace output is not a substitute for deterministic assertions.

references.name: Linear UI redesign
references.borrowed: Reduced visual noise, strong alignment, and dense hierarchy informed the compact card architecture.
references.rejected: The route avoids decorative productivity-app chrome.

references.name: Next.js layouts and pages
references.borrowed: Persistent layout concepts informed same-shell continuity and route handoff behavior.
references.rejected: No Next.js-specific runtime assumption is added to this Vite app.

references.name: Carbon data table style
references.borrowed: Compact rows, subdued toolbar treatment, and dense-but-readable summary rows.
references.rejected: No full data table was used for simple recovery metadata.
